Appearance: At first glance, Flybot 17 appears to be a bulbous, antlike subcompact car — a ite lke a VW bug, A mud/spattered exterior land rusted dents do nothing to improve its looks. Normally, Fybot 17 is driven along the round. However, in the trunk, four folded rotors are stored. These can be temoved, un: folded, and bolted to a shaft which protrudes from the roof 
Function: “ATV” stands for "alhterrain vehi cle” and thats what Flybot 17 i. it was design- ed during a period of budgetary stringency in ‘Alpha Complex, and hence is constructed with the cheapest materials and in the simplest way itis, mirabile dictu, remarkably sturdy and reliable, It is also — so what else is new — remarkably limited. Flybot 17 can travel on land, using its dinky subcompact wheels, However, any’ tr rougher than a superhighway is tough going. The shock absorbers are practically useless, any slope over 15 degrees is impossible Tecan travel on water — like a VW bug, it can float, and, by spinning its wheels rapidly, make some slight headway. However, it can't really steer on wates any suri ikely to swamp it, and in a river itcan do little more than drt ‘And it can travel in the ait. With its rotors attached, Flybot 17 becomes a helicopter, of Sorts Its little micropile engine can tuen the rotors just fast enough to liftoff — ifthe vehi cle is not too heavily loaded.
